committee_roles.label: Product Manager Committee
committee_roles.responsibility: Challenge user value, scope discipline, sequencing, and acceptance criteria before implementation starts.

committee_roles.label: Technical Architect Committee
committee_roles.responsibility: Challenge architecture, interfaces, migration cost, validation strategy, and operational safety.

committee_roles.label: User Committee
committee_roles.responsibility: Challenge the plan from concrete usage scenarios, day-to-day friction, trust, and clarity.

product_context.target_outcome: Turn `autonomous-dev-loop-kit` into a stronger cross-CLI kit that gathers high-quality project data and context before acting, then challenges each version through committee-driven review. Make high-quality data acquisition and committee-driven requirement review first-class parts of the loop, not implicit side effects. Keep the kit usable from minimal prompts such as `循环10`, while preserving strict validation, reporting, and Git publication rules.
constraints: Keep the core protocol shared across Codex CLI and Claude Code CLI.
Use ReAct in the Shunyu Yao paper sense: reason, act, observe, update.
Do not require verbose chain-of-thought in user-visible chat.
Every published version must pass real validation, write a report, and publish to this repo's own GitHub remote.
Favor deterministic scripts and durable repo state over purely prompt-based behavior.

research_findings: The current loop can persist review_state, but write-report.py and publish-iteration.py still treat that artifact as optional, so a version can slip through without a matching recorded review.
The right gate condition is goal-aware: a captured review_state must exist and match the active goal or draft goal when committee review is required by config.
validate-kit should exercise the failure mode directly, otherwise the new gate would live only in documentation and source inspection.
committee_feedback: Product manager committee wants committee review to become an actual release gate, not a best-effort habit that can be skipped under pressure.
Technical architect committee wants one shared helper for review-state enforcement so report and publish reject the same missing-or-stale conditions.
User committee wants clear failure messages that say to run capture-review.py instead of silently producing incomplete reports.
committee_decision: Add a shared require_review_state helper, call it from write-report.py and publish-iteration.py, and make validate-kit assert that report and publish fail when matching review_state is absent.
latest_review_state.goal_title: Require recorded review state before reporting and publication
latest_review_state.current_goal_title: Require recorded review state before reporting and publication

notes: This snapshot is a first-pass repo scan and should be refreshed after major structural changes.
